title: Crepidium oculatum
intro: Crepidium oculatum, also known as the "Eye-Flowered Orchid", is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Crepidium oculatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Crepidium oculatum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Crepidium oculatum is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Crepidium oculatum is classified as a member of the subtribe Cranichidinae. This classification reflects its close relationship with other orchids within the same subtribe. The classification of Crepidium oculatum is used for identification and study purposes.
